---

## Detail Panel Layout, Resizing & Responsiveness

Both detail panels (`AssetDetailPanel.tsx`, `StyleGroupDetailPanel.tsx`) share one
resize/responsive contract, orchestrated from `Index.tsx` via the
`useResizablePanel.ts` hook. Non-obvious details worth knowing before editing:

- **The panel width is a prop, not a hardcoded class.** Both panels take a `width`
  prop (default `408`) and set their own inline width from it. The old hardcoded
  `w-[408px]` / `width: 408` is gone — do not reintroduce it.
- **Draggable divider, desktop only.** At `≥1400px` the panel is docked
  side-by-side and a drag handle sits between the list and the panel; below
  `1400px` the panel overlays absolutely (as before) and the handle is not
  rendered. The `1400px` breakpoint matches the existing `max-[1399px]:` overlay
  classes — keep the two in sync if either changes.
- **Width is clamped and persisted.** Range is `[360, 960]` px, saved to
  `localStorage` under key **`pd-detail-panel-width`**. Double-clicking the handle
  resets to `408`. The list keeps a `min-w-[400px]` floor and the panel wrappers
  are `shrink-0` so the dragged width is honored and the list absorbs the squeeze.
- **Content reflows into two columns when wide.** Above `width ≥ 620` (`WIDE_THRESHOLD`
  in each panel) the scrollable content switches from a single vertical stack to a
  two-column CSS multi-column layout (`break-inside-avoid` per section), the
  single-stack `<Separator />` dividers are hidden, and the hero image is capped
  at 300px tall. If you add a new section, keep it as a direct child of the
  content container so the column flow and break rules apply to it.

Shipped 2026-07-02 (commit `5c266f83`). Frontend-only; no DB/backend involvement.

1	# UI Overview
2	
3	PopDAM is a React + TypeScript single-page application built with Vite, shadcn/ui components, and Tailwind CSS. This document describes every page, panel, and settings tab in the interface.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Application Routes
8	
9	| Route | Component | Purpose |
10	|-------|-----------|---------|
11	| `/` | `Index.tsx` | Main library — browse, search, and tag assets and groups |
12	| `/files` | `FileBrowserPage.tsx` | NAS directory browser (available to all authenticated users) |
13	| `/settings` | `SettingsPage.tsx` | Admin control panel (multiple tabs) |
14	| `/setup` | `SetupPage.tsx` | First-run wizard for initial configuration |
15	| `/downloads` | `DownloadsPage.tsx` | Agent installer download links |
16	| `/login` | `LoginPage.tsx` | Invitation-only sign-in form |
17	| `/ai-tagging/:id` | `AiTaggingDetailPage.tsx` | Detail view for a single AI tagging run |
18	| `/scan-diagnostics` | `ScanDiagnosticsPage.tsx` | Bridge Agent scan history and error details |
19	
20	All routes except `/login` and `/setup` require an authenticated user with at least the `user` role. `/files` is available to all authenticated users (not admin-only). Admin-only settings tabs additionally require the `admin` role.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## NAS File Browser (`/files`)
25	
26	Click-through directory browser that shows the server-side filesystem as seen by the bridge agent. Available to all authenticated users from the top nav.
27	
28	**How it works:** The UI posts a `request-dir-browse` action to admin-api with a path, which writes a `DIR_BROWSE_REQUEST` key to `admin_config`. The bridge agent picks this up on its next heartbeat (within 30s), lists the directory, and posts results back via `report-dir-browse`. The UI polls `get-dir-browse-result` until the `request_id` matches.
29	
30	- Empty path = lists configured scan roots
31	- Click any folder to navigate into it
32	- Back and home buttons for navigation
33	- Shows file sizes and modification dates for regular files
34	- Implemented in `src/components/settings/DirectoryBrowserTab.tsx` (the component) and `src/pages/FileBrowserPage.tsx` (the page wrapper)
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Main Library Page (`/`)
39	
40	The library is the primary working view. It has two top-level modes selectable from the toolbar:
41	
42	- **Groups mode** — browses assets by style group (one card per SKU). This is the default.
43	- **Assets mode** — browses individual files regardless of group membership.
44	
45	Within each mode, two display layouts are available:
46	
47	- **Grid** — thumbnail cards
48	- **List** — compact tabular rows
49	
50	### Library Top Bar (`LibraryTopBar.tsx`)
51	Persistent control bar at the top of the library:
52	- **Search box** — full-text search on filename and tags
53	- **Filter toggle** — shows/hides the left filter sidebar
54	- **Mode toggle** — Groups / Assets
55	- **View toggle** — Grid / List
56	- **Sort control** — sort field and direction (e.g. modified_at, SKU, workflow_status)
57	- **Sync button** — triggers a Bridge Agent scan
58	- **Refresh button** — reloads the current query
59	
60	### Filter Sidebar (`FilterSidebar.tsx`)
61	Faceted filter panel that appears on the left side. Filters include:
62	- Licensor
63	- Property
64	- Workflow status
65	- Asset type
66	- File type (PSD/AI)
67	- Is licensed flag
68	- Tag search
69	- Date range (modified_at or file_created_at)
70	
71	All filter counts update live as other filters change.
72	
73	### Scan Monitor Banner (`ScanMonitorBanner.tsx`)
74	A collapsible progress bar that appears when a Bridge Agent scan is active. Shows:
75	- Files checked, ingested, moved, errors
76	- Current path being scanned
77	- Agent name and scan session ID
78	- Elapsed time
79	
80	### Style Group Grid / List
81	- **`StyleGroupGrid.tsx`** — 4-column responsive grid of cards. Each card shows the primary asset's thumbnail, SKU, asset count, licensor + property name, workflow status badge, and designer conflict badge if applicable.
82	- **`StyleGroupListView.tsx`** — Compact tabular list with the same fields.
83	
84	Clicking a card selects it and opens the Style Group Detail Panel on the right.
85	
86	### Asset Grid / List
87	- **`AssetGrid.tsx`** — thumbnail grid, one card per file
88	- **`AssetListView.tsx`** — tabular list with filename, path, status, dates
89	
90	Clicking a card selects it and opens the Asset Detail Panel on the right.
91	
92	### Pagination Bar (`PaginationBar.tsx`)
93	Shows current page, total records, and navigation buttons. Page size is 200 items.
94	
95	### Bulk Action Bar (`BulkActionBar.tsx`)
96	Appears when one or more groups are selected (multi-select via checkbox). Provides:
97	- Select all on current page
98	- Bulk tag (run AI tagging on all selected groups)
99	- Bulk workflow status change
100	
101	---
102	
103	## Style Group Detail Panel (`StyleGroupDetailPanel.tsx`)
104	
105	Opens on the right side when a style group is selected. Contains the following sections:
106	
107	### 1. Header
108	Group SKU, licensor + property name, close button. Workflow status badge.
109	
110	### 2. Primary Asset Carousel
